redundant-connection.title: Redundant Connection
redundant-connection.description: Given a graph that started as a tree with one extra edge added, find an edge that can be removed so the resulting graph is a tree.

union-find.title: Union Find
union-find.description: A comprehensive guide to the Union Find (Disjoint Set Union) data structure, covering the forest representation, path compression, union by rank, inverse Ackermann analysis, and application patterns for dynamic connectivity problems.

min-cost-to-connect-all-points.title: Min Cost to Connect All Points
min-cost-to-connect-all-points.description: Find the minimum cost to connect all points on a 2D plane using Manhattan distance.

minimum-spanning-tree.title: Minimum Spanning Tree
minimum-spanning-tree.description: A comprehensive guide to Minimum Spanning Trees, covering the cut property, Kruskal's algorithm with Union-Find, Prim's algorithm with a min-heap, and the trade-offs between the two approaches.

cheapest-flights-within-k-stops.title: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops
cheapest-flights-within-k-stops.description: Find the cheapest price from a source city to a destination with at most k stops.

network-delay-time.title: Network Delay Time
network-delay-time.description: Find the minimum time for a signal to reach all nodes in a network of directed weighted edges.

path-with-maximum-probability.title: Path with Maximum Probability
path-with-maximum-probability.description: Find the path with the maximum probability of success between two nodes in an undirected weighted graph.

path-with-minimum-effort.title: Path With Minimum Effort
path-with-minimum-effort.description: Find the route from top-left to bottom-right of a grid that minimizes the maximum absolute height difference between consecutive cells.

swim-in-rising-water.title: Swim in Rising Water
swim-in-rising-water.description: Find the minimum time to swim from the top-left to the bottom-right of a grid where the water level rises over time.

shortest-path.title: Shortest Path
shortest-path.description: A comprehensive guide to shortest path algorithms, covering Dijkstra's algorithm with a min-heap, the Bellman-Ford algorithm for graphs with negative weights, and their variations on grids and constrained problems.

cracking-the-safe.title: Cracking the Safe
cracking-the-safe.description: Find a minimum-length string that contains every possible n-digit password over a k-symbol alphabet as a substring.

extra.technique: Eulerian Circuit (Hierholzer's Algorithm on De Bruijn Graph)

reconstruct-itinerary.title: Reconstruct Itinerary
reconstruct-itinerary.description: Given a list of airline tickets represented as pairs of departure and arrival airports, reconstruct the itinerary in order starting from JFK.

eulerian-circuit.title: Eulerian Circuit
eulerian-circuit.description: A comprehensive guide to Eulerian circuits and paths, covering the necessary and sufficient conditions for their existence, Hierholzer's algorithm, De Bruijn sequences, and applications to graph traversal problems.

climbing-stairs.title: Climbing Stairs
climbing-stairs.description: Given a staircase with n steps where you can climb one or two steps at a time, determine the number of distinct ways to reach the top.

house-robber.title: House Robber
house-robber.description: Given an array representing money in each house along a street, find the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.

house-robber-II.title: House Robber II
house-robber-II.description: Given an array representing money in houses arranged in a circle, find the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.

extra.technique: Circular constraint reduction to linear subproblems

min-cost-climbing-stairs.title: Min Cost Climbing Stairs
min-cost-climbing-stairs.description: Given an array of step costs, find the minimum cost to reach the top of the staircase, starting from step 0 or step 1, climbing one or two steps at a time.

dp-foundations-1d-dp.title: DP Foundations & 1D DP
dp-foundations-1d-dp.description: A comprehensive guide to dynamic programming: optimal substructure, overlapping subproblems, memoization vs tabulation, state definition, recurrence relations, and space optimization, taught through one-dimensional DP problems.

coin-change.title: Coin Change
coin-change.description: Given coin denominations and a target amount, find the fewest number of coins needed to make up that amount.

coin-change-ii.title: Coin Change II
coin-change-ii.description: Given coin denominations and a target amount, count the number of distinct combinations that sum to that amount.

last-stone-weight-ii.title: Last Stone Weight II
last-stone-weight-ii.description: Given an array of stone weights, smash stones together optimally to minimize the weight of the last remaining stone.

extra.technique: 0/1 Knapsack with value maximization and partition minimization

partition-equal-subset-sum.title: Partition Equal Subset Sum
partition-equal-subset-sum.description: Given an integer array, determine whether it can be partitioned into two subsets with equal sum.

perfect-squares.title: Perfect Squares
perfect-squares.description: Given an integer n, find the least number of perfect square numbers that sum to n.

extra.technique: Unbounded Knapsack with generated item set and minimization

target-sum.title: Target Sum
target-sum.description: Given an integer array and a target, count the number of ways to assign + and - signs to each element so that the expression evaluates to the target.

extra.technique: 0/1 Knapsack with subset sum transformation and counting

knapsack-dp.title: Knapsack DP
knapsack-dp.description: A comprehensive guide to knapsack dynamic programming: the 0/1 knapsack pattern for bounded items and the unbounded knapsack pattern for unlimited items, with the critical inner loop direction insight that distinguishes them.

longest-increasing-subsequence.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
longest-increasing-subsequence.description: Given an integer array, find the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.

number-of-longest-increasing-subsequence.title: Number of Longest Increasing Subsequence
number-of-longest-increasing-subsequence.description: Given an integer array, return the number of longest strictly increasing subsequences.

extra.technique: LIS counting variant: length + count arrays

russian-doll-envelopes.title: Russian Doll Envelopes
russian-doll-envelopes.description: Given a set of envelopes with width and height, find the maximum number of envelopes that can be nested inside each other.

extra.technique: Dimensional reduction + O(n log n) LIS via patience sorting

longest-increasing-subsequence-dp.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence DP
longest-increasing-subsequence-dp.description: A comprehensive guide to the Longest Increasing Subsequence problem: the O(n²) DP formulation, the O(n log n) patience sorting optimization, counting and reconstruction variants, and the unifying view through Dilworth's theorem.

burst-balloons.title: Burst Balloons
burst-balloons.description: Burst all balloons to collect the maximum coins, where bursting a balloon yields the product of its value and the values of its current neighbours.

extra.technique: Interval DP choosing the last balloon burst in each range

cherry-pickup.title: Cherry Pickup
cherry-pickup.description: Collect the maximum number of cherries on an n x n grid by travelling from the top-left corner to the bottom-right and back, avoiding thorns.

extra.technique: Multi-dimensional grid DP modelling the round trip as two simultaneous walkers

count-square-submatrices-with-all-ones.title: Count Square Submatrices with All Ones
count-square-submatrices-with-all-ones.description: Given a matrix of zeros and ones, count how many square submatrices are composed entirely of ones, including all sizes.

extra.technique: Matrix-local DP anchoring the largest square at each bottom-right cell

maximum-number-of-points-with-cost.title: Maximum Number of Points with Cost
maximum-number-of-points-with-cost.description: Pick one cell in each row of a matrix to maximize the total value, paying a penalty equal to the horizontal distance between cells chosen in consecutive rows.

extra.technique: Row-by-row grid DP with prefix and suffix running maxima to linearize the transition

maximum-profit-in-job-scheduling.title: Maximum Profit in Job Scheduling
maximum-profit-in-job-scheduling.description: Given jobs with start times, end times, and profits, select a non-overlapping subset that maximizes total profit.

extra.technique: Weighted interval scheduling DP over a sorted timeline with binary search

minimum-path-sum.title: Minimum Path Sum
minimum-path-sum.description: Given an m x n grid of non-negative numbers, find a path from top-left to bottom-right, moving only down or right, that minimizes the sum of numbers along the path.

extra.technique: Grid DP minimizing path cost with down and right moves

triangle.title: Triangle
triangle.description: Given a triangular array of numbers, return the minimum path sum from the top to the bottom, moving to an adjacent number on the row below at each step.

extra.technique: Grid DP minimizing path sum on a triangular grid

unique-paths-ii.title: Unique Paths II
unique-paths-ii.description: Count the number of distinct paths a robot can take from the top-left to the bottom-right corner of a grid, moving only down or right, while avoiding obstacles.

2d-grid-dp.title: 2D Grid DP
2d-grid-dp.description: A comprehensive guide to dynamic programming on two-dimensional grids: path counting, minimum and maximum path sums, obstacle variants, matrix-local recurrences, transition optimization, multi-dimensional grid states, and rolling-array space reduction.
